title: Archived popover cache can expose another user's archived page list
reasoning: The archived data cache is module-scoped and not keyed by current user, session, or permissions. On reopen, the component renders cached pages and cached authorization flags immediately while the fresh /api/archived request is still loading. In a long-lived browser session where the authenticated user changes, a later user can temporarily see the previous user's archived page titles and admin/lead affordances before the new fetch completes. Server-side action authorization may block mutations, but the page metadata has already been disclosed in the UI.
reproduction: As an admin/user A, open the archived popover so cachedArchived is populated. Change authentication to user B in the same SPA/runtime, then open the archived popover; the previous cache is used until /api/archived returns.
recommendation: Keep the cache component-local or key it by authenticated user and permission context. Alternatively clear cachedArchived on auth changes and show the skeleton while a fresh request is pending instead of rendering stale cross-session data.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests are TipTapRenderer-only and do not cover ArchivedContent caching, user changes, or stale authorization data.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that seeds one user's archived response, rerenders/open as another user before the second request resolves, and asserts the first user's pages and permission buttons are not rendered.
minimumFixScope: ArchivedContent cache ownership and loading behavior around /api/archived data.
